title: Two Sum
prompt:
## Two Sum

Read two space-separated integers `a` and `b` from stdin and print their sum.
input_format: Single line containing two integers separated by a space.
hints: [{"afterFailures":2,"text":"Read the line, split on whitespace, cast to int."},{"afterFailures":4,"text":"Use `input().split()` then `map(int, ...)`."}]

title: Reverse a String
prompt:
## Reverse a String

Read a single line from stdin and print it reversed.
output_format: The same characters in reverse order.

title: Valid Parentheses
prompt:
## Valid Parentheses

Given a string containing only `()[]{}`, decide whether the brackets are balanced.

Print `YES` if every opener has a matching closer in the correct order, otherwise `NO`.
input_format: A single line containing only the characters `()[]{}`.
hints: [{"afterFailures":2,"text":"A stack is your friend — push opens, pop on closes."},{"afterFailures":4,"text":"On every close, the popped opener must match the close's pair."}]
test_cases: [{"input":"()[]{}","output":"YES","explanation":"All openers have an immediate match.","is_sample":true},{"input":"([)]","output":"NO","explanation":"Pairs cross; not balanced.","is_sample":false},{"input":"{[()]}","output":"YES","explanation":"Nested correctly.","is_sample":false}]

title: LRU Cache Simulation
prompt:
## LRU Cache

Simulate a Least-Recently-Used cache of fixed capacity.

The first line contains capacity `c` and number of operations `q`. The next `q` lines are either `PUT k v` or `GET k`. For each `GET`, print the value, or `-1` if the key is absent.
input_format: Line 1: c q. Next q lines: PUT k v | GET k.
output_format: One line per GET — the value or -1.
hints: [{"afterFailures":3,"text":"Touching a key (GET or PUT) makes it most-recent."},{"afterFailures":6,"text":"A hash map + doubly-linked list gives O(1) per op."}]
